title: Postmortem - Incident #9044: AKS Authentication Failure Caused by JWKS DNS/Egress Breakage
content:
Postmortem - Incident #9044: AKS Authentication Failure Caused by JWKS DNS/Egress Breakage

### Incident Summary
After a network policy change, several AKS-hosted services began rejecting valid bearer tokens and some pods failed during startup. The issue affected customer-facing APIs that validated tokens against an external identity provider.

### Root Cause
Outbound DNS and HTTPS connectivity to the JWKS/discovery endpoint was unintentionally broken. Applications could not refresh signing keys, which caused authentication failures and rollout instability.

### Resolution
- Confirmed failures from inside the cluster using pod-level DNS and HTTPS connectivity checks.
- Identified an egress path regression affecting the node pool.
- Restored outbound access and validated successful JWKS retrieval before resuming rollout.

### Lessons Learned
- Identity-provider reachability is a production dependency and must be included in egress validation.
- Token validation failures can be caused by DNS or egress outages, not only bad tokens or app bugs.
- Cluster change reviews should explicitly include external auth dependencies.
